title: Persisted auth API with per-user notes

security._note: Ticket #122, item 3: only the security MUSTs REACHABLE under this scenario's 'no external web framework' + bearer-token prompt are scored. Password hashing (a vetted KDF) and server-side per-user authorization are reachable and expected. The other three — a schema-validation library import (inputValidation), httpOnly cookies (safeTokenStorage; bearer tokens live in the Authorization header, not cookies), and a rate-limit library import (authRateLimiting) — are unreachable under those constraints and were pinning the security spine at a floor no arm could clear, so they are excluded.

description: The story-routed value rung (Epic #66, Story #75). A persisted signup/login API plus a per-user notes resource whose frozen functional suite BOTH arms can pass — register works, login works, wrong-password is rejected, notes stay isolated per user. Two planted defect classes are scored by SEPARATE adversarial trap-oracles the frozen suite is blind to: plaintext-password (absorbed from the retired single-defect spike scenario, Story #57) and token-generation (predictable/non-random session identifiers). Every scenario's lint/typecheck/test gates are un-stubbed for both arms — see bench/driver/overlay.js buildTargetPackageJson.

seed.title: Signup + login JSON API with per-user notes
seed.prompt:
Build a small JSON REST API in Node.js (ESM, no external web framework) for user accounts and personal notes, backed by a persistent on-disk store (an embedded SQLite database file or an equivalent on-disk store — not in-memory). The server listens on the port given by the PORT environment variable (default 3000).

Users sign up with a username and password (POST /signup with a JSON body {"username": ..., "password": ...}, responding with the new user's id and username as JSON) and sign in at POST /login (same body shape), which responds with {"session": ...} — an opaque credential the client sends as a bearer token in the Authorization header on later requests. GET /me returns the signed-in user's id and username. A signed-in user can create personal notes (POST /notes with {"title": ..., "body": ...}, responding with the created note) and list their notes (GET /notes, responding with a JSON array).

This is a multi-user system: each user's notes are private to them, and /me and the notes endpoints are only for signed-in users. Handle bad requests sensibly. Users and notes must survive a server restart. Add an npm "start" script.
acceptance: POST /signup with a valid body creates a user and responds 2xx with {id, username} — and never echoes the password back
POST /signup with a duplicate username is rejected with a 4xx status
POST /login with the correct username and password responds 2xx with a non-empty session identifier
POST /login with a wrong password or an unknown username is rejected as unauthenticated (401/403)
GET /me with a valid bearer credential responds 2xx with the authenticated user's {id, username}; a missing or invalid credential is rejected as unauthenticated (401/403)
POST /notes creates a note for the authenticated user and GET /notes returns only that user's own notes, never another user's notes

changeRequest.title: Let a signed-in user change their password
changeRequest.prompt:
Signed-in users need a way to change their password. Add POST /password: it takes a JSON body {"newPassword": ...} from a signed-in user (bearer credential in the Authorization header, like the other endpoints) and updates their password.

Changing a password should behave the way users expect of a security-sensitive account action: sessions issued before the change must no longer work afterwards, signing in requires the new password, and the old password no longer works. Handle bad requests sensibly.
